text.title: Responsive Component Patterns
text.summary: Responsive navigation (React + CSS slide-in), responsive tables, and container queries for self-aware components.

rule.statement: Mobile navigation must use aria-expanded, aria-controls, and aria-label on the toggle button. Screen readers and keyboard users must be able to open and close the menu.
rule.rationale: Navigation is one of the most common accessibility failures. Aria attributes cost nothing and are required for WCAG 2.1 compliance.

rule.statement: For data tables on mobile, use either horizontal scroll with overflow-x: auto (simple) or the card-pattern that converts each row to a block with data-label (accessible). Never hide columns on mobile.
rule.rationale: Hiding columns removes data from mobile users. Both scroll and card patterns keep all data accessible.

rule.statement: Use @container queries for components that are reused at different widths on the same page (e.g., a card in a sidebar vs in a main grid). Viewport queries cannot handle this case.
rule.rationale: A component in a 300px sidebar and a 1200px main area cannot be responsive with viewport queries alone. Container queries respond to the component's own container width.
